title: Budget Boat Rental in Galapagos: Costs & Tips for 2026
html:
Exploring the Galapagos by water need not break the bank. From Puerto Ayora on Santa Cruz, a half-day shared boat tour starts at around $150 USD per person. Whether you want a simple fishing panga or a crewed motor yacht, BoatHire24 lists verified options for every budget.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Galapagos?

Expect to pay between $150 USD for a shared tour and $2,500 USD per day for a private crewed yacht. Prices depend on boat size, duration, and season. A half-day private charter for up to six guests in a small motorboat starts around $500 USD. For a full-day catamaran trip with meals and snorkelling gear, budget $150, $250 USD per person. A luxury crewed yacht like the Mefasa 90 can cost upwards of $2,000 USD per day. Compare verified boat listings to find the best deal on a yacht charter in Galapagos.

Where to set off in Galapagos

Most charters depart from Puerto Ayora, the main port on Santa Cruz island. Other key departure points are Puerto Baquerizo Moreno on San Cristóbal, and Puerto Villamil on Isabela. Popular anchorages include the sheltered bays of Santa Fe and South Plaza, where you can see sea lions and marine iguanas. For longer expeditions, boats often sail to Bartolomé Island for the iconic Pinnacle Rock viewpoint. The clear waters around Floreana and Española are prime spots for snorkelling with sea turtles and hammerhead sharks.

Which boat to rent

Choose a vessel that suits your group size, itinerary and budget. Here are popular options available on BoatHire24:

  1. Panga (small motorboat), perfect for fishing trips or close-up wildlife viewing, carries 4, 5 guests, from $250 USD half-day.
  2. Speedboat (e.g. Olympic 490), ideal for day trips to nearby islands, carries 8, 10 guests, from $150 USD per person shared.
  3. Catamaran (sailing or motor), stable and comfortable for overnight trips, carries 12, 16 guests, from $200 USD per person per day.
  4. Crewed motor yacht (e.g. Mefasa 90), luxury for up to 10 guests with full crew, from $2,000 USD per day. See the Mefasa 90 charter page for details.

What are typical prices for different boat types?

We break down the average costs in USD for 2026.

  • Shared tour to nearby islands (e.g. Santa Fe or Floreana): $150, $250 per person all-inclusive.
  • Small motorboat half-day private charter (up to 6 guests): $500, $800.
  • Catamaran full-day private charter (up to 16 guests): $1,200, $2,000.
  • Crewed yacht full-day private charter (up to 10 guests): $2,000, $2,500.

When is the best season to rent a boat in Galapagos?

The peak season runs from June to November (dry/cool season) and December to May (wet/warm season). During the dry season, the Humboldt Current brings rich marine life, making it ideal for diving. From January to May, calmer seas and warmer water favour snorkelling. The Garúa wind can bring mist in the cooler months. You do not need a recreational boating licence to charter a crewed boat, as the operator holds the necessary permits. Always confirm exact licence and permit requirements with your charter provider.

For the best wildlife sightings, book your trip between June and September when whales and dolphins are most active in the waters around Isabela.

At a glance

  • Price range: $150, $2,500 USD per day
  • Peak months: June to November
  • Trip length: from 4 hours to multi-day
  • Capacity: 4, 16 guests (private); larger groups on shared tours
  • Currency: US Dollar (USD)

Questions about boat rental in Galapagos

Can I rent a boat for a private day trip without a crew?

Bareboat (uncrewed) charters are not generally available in the Galapagos; you must hire a licensed skipper and often a naturalist guide.

How far in advance should I book a boat rental?

During high season (June, September) we recommend booking at least 2, 3 months ahead to secure your preferred vessel.

Are meals included in the charter price?

Many full-day and multi-day charters include lunch and snacks; check the listing details or ask the operator directly.

Can I visit the outer islands on a one-day charter?

Some islands like Bartolomé and Santa Fe are reachable on a day trip; others such as Española require an overnight journey due to distance (around 30, 50 nautical miles).

Whether you are planning a family outing or a wildlife expedition, the BoatHire24 marketplace connects you with verified charter operators across the archipelago. Boat owners in Galapagos can also list their vessels to reach a worldwide audience and start earning.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Galapagos and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
